changes.before: Not strong enough to stop the market. Strong enough.
changes.after: Strong enough.

changes.before: Not at once. Quietly.
changes.after: Quietly.

changes.before: Not a cure. A pause.
changes.after: A pause.

changes.before: Not water. Water would have struck harder and stranger, day to day.
changes.after: Water would have struck harder and stranger, day to day.

changes.before: Not stubborn now. Shaken.
changes.after: The color had gone out of his face.

changes.before: Not stubborn now. Shaken.

changes.before: Not the thinned trace she had read in living bodies.
This was not the grain's slow rot.
It was not difficult work, only careful work.
changes.after: It was careful work.

changes.before: His gaze went from elven ears to Farstrider armor to swords, and his mouth flattened.
changes.after: He took in elven ears, Farstrider armor, then swords, and his mouth flattened.

changes.before: hands already starting to point behind him before he seemed to realize he meant to.
changes.after: already pointing behind him before he caught himself.

changes.before: the weight of the next door waiting to open

changes.before: His eyes jumped from the oil-dark wall to the arrow to the sacks.
changes.after: He tracked from the oil-dark wall to the arrow to the sacks.

changes.before: anger finally pointed somewhere useful
changes.after: angry now at the granary instead of them

changes.before: Her gaze went over the mouth, the throat, the hands.
changes.after: She checked the mouth, the throat, the hands.

changes.before: the place the road feeds

changes.before: the next answer was already moving

changes.before: Stalls stood open on three sides of the square. A woman crossed between them with a basket on her arm. A boy dragged one muddy boot behind him. A man argued with a carter over a crate.
changes.after: Stalls stood open on three sides of the square, a woman crossing between them with a basket on her arm while a boy dragged one muddy boot behind him and a man argued with a carter over a crate.

changes.before: He hesitated, then nodded once.
Jaina fed a thin stabilizing ward through it and watched the stain stop where it was.
changes.after: Jaina fed a thin stabilizing ward through it, and the stain stopped where it was.

changes.before: She wasn't driving him. She was making room and letting him talk himself into the answer.
changes.after: She let the silence sit until he filled it.

changes.before: That answered enough by itself.

changes.before: Village man before militia captain.

changes.before: He was buying time.
changes.after: He said nothing.

changes.before: She had the look of someone already moving three steps past a discovery.
changes.after: She was already looking to Jaina for the rest of it.

changes.before: Jaina felt the old scrap from Millhaven sharpen into something harder.
